verdicts.issue: title field contains HTML tag 'BinFI'; the real title is 'BinFI: an efficient fault injector for safety-critical machine learning systems' (split across title='BinFI' + subtitle). HTML/XML tags in title render literally in citations.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: set title = {{BinFI}: An Efficient Fault Injector for Safety-Critical Machine Learning Systems} and remove the subtitle field. No .qmd propagation needed (key unchanged).

verdicts.issue: duplicate of chen2020simple — both entries are the SimCLR paper (Chen, Kornblith, Norouzi, Hinton; ICML 2020, PMLR vol 119, pp. 1597-1607). Two keys for the same work cause inconsistent citations and split bibliography.

suggested_fix.note: Choose one canonical key (chen2020simple has cleaner ICML/PMLR metadata). Delete chen2020simclr and rewrite every [@chen2020simclr] / @chen2020simclr citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd to chen2020simple.

verdicts.issue: Note: this is the canonical version (PMLR vol 119, pp. 1597-1607). Its sibling chen2020simclr is the duplicate to remove.

title field is just 'CLEAR'; the real title 'CLEAR: Cross-Layer Exploration for Architecting Resilience' is split across title + subtitle, and the subtitle contains HTML <u> tags around individual letters (<u>C</u>ross<u>-L</u>ayer...). Renders awkwardly. Worth a cleanup pass but the bib entry is otherwise correct.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: title = {{CLEAR}: Cross-Layer Exploration for Architecting Resilience --- Combining Hardware and Software Techniques to Tolerate Soft Errors in Processor Cores}; remove subtitle. No .qmd changes.

verdicts.issue: author = {Anthropic} but the Constitutional AI paper byline lists 50+ named authors with Yuntao Bai as first author (Bai, Yuntao and Kadavath, Saurav and Kundu, Sandipan et al.). Per §4a, full author names should be present where attested. Also, two separate entries exist for the same paper: claude2022constitutional (this) and bai2022constitutional (chunk 1).

suggested_fix.note: Either expand author list to the published byline (Bai, Yuntao and Kadavath, Saurav et al.) and treat claude2022constitutional as the canonical key, OR consolidate with bai2022constitutional and rewrite citations in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd. Recommend keeping bai2022constitutional and removing claude2022constitutional.

verdicts.issue: no DOI/URL; journal 'Global Water Intelligence' issue 10/10. Hard to independently verify but the metadata is internally consistent and the journal exists.

key year 2015 vs date field 2016 — IEEE Comm. Surveys & Tutorials article published Q1 2016 (DOI 10.1109/comst.2015.2481183 was assigned 2015 when accepted). Diff is 1 year (within §5 tolerance). Also: journal field contains '&' HTML entity instead of literal '\&' or '&' — renders as 'IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials' in the bibliography.

suggested_fix.note: Replace '&' with '\&' in journal field. No .qmd changes.

verdicts.issue: duplicate of dean2012large — the two entries have identical author lists, year, journal, booktitle, publisher, volume, pages, URL, source. Same paper (DistBelief / Large Scale Distributed Deep Networks, NeurIPS 2012).

suggested_fix.note: Choose one canonical key (recommend dean2012distbelief, which is more semantically descriptive). Delete dean2012large and rewrite every [@dean2012large] / @dean2012large citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd to dean2012distbelief.

verdicts.issue: duplicate of dean2012distbelief (same paper, byte-for-byte identical except for the key).

suggested_fix.note: Delete this entry; consolidate citations to dean2012distbelief in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: key year 2021 vs entry year 2020 — ACM SIGKDD 2020 paper (DOI 10.1145/3394486.3406703). Diff is 1 (within §5 tolerance) but the key 'deepspeed_training_system_2021' is unusual: contains underscores (unconventional for this corpus, though not invalid) and the year token is 2021 while the work is 2020. Also title='DeepSpeed' with the real subtitle 'System Optimizations Enable Training Deep Learning Models with Over 100 Billion Parameters' split into subtitle field; journal/booktitle contain '&' HTML entity.

suggested_fix.note: Optional rename to rasley2020deepspeed (matches first author + actual year + topic, follows the corpus convention). If renamed, update every [@deepspeed_training_system_2021] / @deepspeed_training_system_2021 citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd. Also fix '&' -> '\&' and consolidate title in-place.

verdicts.issue: Note: this is the arXiv version. devlin2019bert below is the NAACL 2019 conference version of the same paper. Two keys are intentional (preprint vs. proceedings) but worth confirming both are actually cited; if not, drop the unused one.

DOI 10.1109/TMC.2022.3180000 prefix is the IEEE TMC pattern but the paper title 'Pruning and Sparse Training for On-Device Neural Network Optimization' did not surface in spot checks against IEEE Xplore for that DOI. The DOI may resolve to a different paper (TMC 2022 with that DOI is by other authors). Worth re-verifying with a live URL fetch or DOI lookup.
Capitalized key 'Dwork2006Theory' breaks the corpus's all-lowercase key convention. Cosmetic only.

suggested_fix.note: Optional rename for key-style consistency. If renamed, update every [@Dwork2006Theory] / @Dwork2006Theory citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: Capitalized key 'Elsken2019' breaks the corpus's all-lowercase key convention; also the key has no topic word (just surname+year). Cosmetic.

suggested_fix.note: Optional rename for key-style consistency (lowercase + topic word). If renamed, update every [@Elsken2019] / @Elsken2019 citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: first-author surname 'Sevilla' does not match key prefix 'epoch'. This is an intentional project-name key (Epoch AI / Compute Trends report) and is exempt per §5 (corporate-style key for an organization-attributed work).

author='Parliament, European and of the European Union, Council' is a malformed BibTeX author parse — should be authored to a corporate entity, e.g., '{European Parliament and Council of the European Union}' (curly braces protect the corporate name from BibTeX's surname-splitting). Cosmetic; renders weirdly today.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: author = {{European Parliament and Council of the European Union}}. No .qmd changes.

verdicts.issue: Title 'Sustainable Digital Markets Act: Environmental Transparency in AI' does not match a real EU regulation that I could verify; the Digital Markets Act (DMA) is a separate 2022 instrument with no environmental-transparency provision under that name. The note links to https://ec.europa.eu/digital-strategy (a generic landing page, not a specific document). May be a fabricated or mislabeled entry. Also: capitalized key violates corpus convention.

suggested_fix.note: Manually verify whether this entry refers to a real EU document. If real, replace title + URL with the actual instrument; if not, delete the entry and remove citations from book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: key year 2018 (CVPR year) vs entry year 2017 (arXiv year). Within §5 tolerance (diff=1) so the key/content check passes. But the entry only points to the arXiv preprint; the canonical citation is the CVPR 2018 paper 'Robust Physical-World Attacks on Deep Learning Visual Classification' (note the 'Visual Classification' suffix in the published title — the bib title is the older arXiv title). Either upgrade to the CVPR version (Eykholt et al., CVPR 2018, pp. 1625-1634, DOI 10.1109/CVPR.2018.00175) or rename the key to eykholt2017robust to match the arXiv year.

suggested_fix.note: Recommend upgrading to the CVPR 2018 metadata (title='Robust Physical-World Attacks on Deep Learning Visual Classification', booktitle='IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)', year=2018, pages='1625--1634', publisher='IEEE') in-place. No .qmd changes if key is preserved.

verdicts.issue: no year field (only date=2021-06-11); key has no year token at all. Renders as '(Nguyen et al., n.d.)' or similar in some citation styles. Real publication: Nguyen et al., 'Federated Learning with Buffered Asynchronous Aggregation', AISTATS 2022 (PMLR vol 151). Bib labels it as 'Proceedings of Machine Learning Research (AISTATS)' but doesn't pin a year.

suggested_fix.note: Add year=2022; optionally rename key to nguyen2022fedbuff (corpus convention: surnameYearTopic). If renamed, update every [@fedbuff] / @fedbuff citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: no year field (only date=2024-05-07); key has no year token. Same pattern as fedbuff above — short keyword key without year is below the corpus convention.

suggested_fix.note: Add year=2024; optionally rename key to rodio2024fedstale. If renamed, update every [@fedstale] / @fedstale citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: no year field (only date=2024-06-05); key has no year token. Same pattern as fedbuff/fedstale.

suggested_fix.note: Add year=2024; optionally rename key to ma2024fedstaleweight. If renamed, update every [@fedstaleweight] / @fedstaleweight citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: Minor: entry year=2021 (arXiv preprint year) but key year=2022 (JMLR publication year). Diff=1, within §5 tolerance. The journal/volume/number metadata (JMLR vol 23 no 120) is the 2022 final publication; only the year field is set to the preprint year. Cosmetic — recommend year=2022 to match the journal metadata.
Capitalized key violates corpus convention; also the key has no topic word.

suggested_fix.note: Optional rename for consistency. If renamed, update every [@Feurer2015] / @Feurer2015 citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: duplicate of forti2020ewaste — both entries are the UN University 'Global E-waste Monitor 2020' report by Forti, Bald{\'e}, Kuehr, Bel. Slight wording differences in title ('Quantities, Flows, and Circular Economy Potential' vs 'Quantities, Flows and the Circular Economy Potential') but it is the same publication. Also: capitalized key.

suggested_fix.note: Choose one (recommend forti2020ewaste — lowercase, has topic word, uses @report which matches the document type better than @book). Delete Forti2020 and rewrite every [@Forti2020] / @Forti2020 citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd to forti2020ewaste.

verdicts.issue: Note: this is the canonical version. Its sibling Forti2020 is the duplicate to remove.

author = 'Incorporated, Framework Computer' is a malformed BibTeX author parse — should be a corporate entity, e.g., '{Framework Computer Inc.}'. Renders as 'Incorporated, F. C.' in many styles. Title 'Modular Laptops: A New Approach to Sustainable Computing' is not a real Framework publication that I could verify; the URL https://frame.work/ is the company landing page, not a specific document. Effectively a citation to a company website with a made-up document title.

suggested_fix.note: Either point url to a real Framework publication (e.g., a sustainability whitepaper) and adjust the title accordingly, or convert this to a generic '@misc' for the company website. Fix author to {{Framework Computer Inc.}}.

verdicts.issue: Minor: actual venue is ICLR 2023 (paper accepted; OpenReview ID Lqs-Nm9kE_2). Bib has booktitle = 'International Conference on Learning Representations' (correct but should be expanded per §2 to 'International Conference on Learning Representations (ICLR)') and publisher should be 'OpenReview.net' per §3. Currently no publisher set.
Minor: booktitle contains '&' HTML entity ('Knowledge Discovery & Data Mining') — should be '\&'.
duplicate of gdpr2016regulation — both entries are the EU GDPR (Regulation 2016/679, OJEU L 119, 2016-05-04). Identical URL, identical author, identical year.

suggested_fix.note: Choose one (recommend gdpr2016 — shorter, more @regulation-typed). Delete gdpr2016regulation and rewrite every [@gdpr2016regulation] / @gdpr2016regulation citation in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd to gdpr2016.

verdicts.issue: duplicate of gdpr2016 (same EU regulation).

suggested_fix.note: Delete this entry; consolidate citations to gdpr2016 in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: Minor: per §2 venue expansion, booktitle should be 'ACM Symposium on Operating Systems Principles (SOSP)'. Currently has the lowercase autogenerated DOI title.

author = 'Institute, World Resources and for Sustainable Development, World Business Council' is a malformed BibTeX author parse — should be two corporate entities: '{World Resources Institute} and {World Business Council for Sustainable Development}'. Renders as 'Institute, W. R. and for Sustainable Development, W. B. C.' in many styles.

suggested_fix.note: Fix in-place: author = {{World Resources Institute} and {World Business Council for Sustainable Development}}. No .qmd changes.
